CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-20-2025 Continued To May-28-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Apr-14-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. BRIAN LANCE, Esq. of Abbey, Weitzenberg, Warren & Emery, P.C. (tel: 707-542-5050) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. ELTON RUSHING, Esq. of Hassard Bonnington LLP (tel: 415-288-9800) for Defendant Sutter Bay Hospitals dba California Pacific Medical Center.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS LAKEPORT POST ACUTE, LLC, PROVIDENCE GROUP NH, LLC, PACS HOLDINGS, LLC, AND PACS GROUP INC.'s MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

Matter not argued / contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion to compel arbitration/denied.

1. CCP 1295 and Ruiz v. Podolsky (2010) 50 Cal.4th 838 [compelling non signatories to arbitrate medical malpractice claim] do not apply to this case because the gravamen of plaintiffs' claims is elder abuse/neglect.

2. "If the primary basis for the wrongful death claim sounds in professional negligence
as defined by MICRA, then section 1295 applies. If, as plaintiffs claim here, the
primary basis is under the Elder Abuse and Dependent Adult Civil Protection Act
(Welf. & Inst. Code, Sec. 15600 et seq.) (the Act), then section 1295 does not apply
and neither does Ruiz 's exception to the general rule that one who has not
consented cannot be compelled to arbitrate." (Avila v. Southern California Specialty Care, Inc. (2018) 20 Cal.App.5th 835, 842.) Plaintiffs (heirs) never agreed to arbitrate their claims.

3. CCP 1281.2(c) [conflicting rulings] applies to this action since the gravamen of the claims is elder abuse. The court denies arbitration of decedent's survivor claims because there is a possibility of conflicting rulings with plaintiffs' wrongful death claims and their claims against Sutter Bay Hospitals, who is not required to arbitrate. (CCP 1281.2(d); Daniel v. Sunrise Senior Living, Inc. (2013) 212 Cal.App.4th 674, 686 [denying arbitration where there was a possibility of conflicting rulings between the survivor and wrongful death claims].)

Order submitted.

Judge: Barbara Zuniga; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/BZ)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-14-2025 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. BRIAN LANCE, Esq. of Abbey, Weitzenberg, Warren & Emery, P.C. (tel: 707-542-5050) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. ELTON RUSHING, Esq. of Hassard Bonnington LLP (tel: 415-288-9800) for Defendant Sutter Bay Hospitals dba California Pacific Medical Center.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS, DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint/Overruled.

1. Per CCP 452, a court liberally construes the pleadings.
2. The use of the term "DEFENDANTS" does not render the First Amended Complaint uncertain.
3. "Demurrers for uncertainty are disfavored and are granted only if the pleading is so incomprehensible that a defendant cannot reasonably respond." (A.J. Fistes Corp. v. GDI. Best Contractors, Inc. (2019) 38 Cal.App.5th 677, 695.)
4. Sutter can reasonably respond and raises factual issues for discovery. The court notes that plaintiffs allege when decedent was in Sutter's custody and when she developed an ulcer there. (FAC, pars. 9, 11-13, and 19.) The court further finds that causes of action two for willful misconduct and three for elder abuse are alleged with sufficient particularity.

Order submitted.

Judge: Barbara Zuniga; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/BZ)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-14-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. BRIAN LANCE, Esq. of Abbey, Weitzenberg, Warren & Emery, P.C. (tel: 707-542-5050) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. ELTON RUSHING, Esq. of Hassard Bonnington LLP (tel: 415-288-9800) for Defendant Sutter Bay Hospitals dba California Pacific Medical Center.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS, DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion to strike/Denied.
1. The prayer for attorney's fees is proper based on the elder abuse claim. (See Welfare & Institutions Code sec. 15657(a).)
2. The prayer for punitive damages without compliance with CCP 425.13 is also proper based on the elder abuse claim. (Covenant Care, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 790 ["Where the gravamen of an action is violation of the Elder Abuse Act, Central Pathology's rationale for applying section 425.13 to the common law intentional torts at issue in that case does not obtain."].)

Order submitted.

Judge: Barbara Zuniga; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/BZ)
